source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
title: Ensure Audit Logging is Enabled

input_row.source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
input_row.title: Ensure Audit Logging is Enabled
input_row.description: This control is important because it helps ensure activity within the cluster and identifies who has last modified the document and who has access to it, in case of breaches. It also ensures compliance with regulation requirements.
input_row.rationale: Impact: Reduces risks of any fraud since worker activity is being monitored and tracked.
input_row.audit: 1. Sign into the AWS Management Console • Sign into the AWS Management Console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ with your AWS account credentials. 2. Open the Amazon Neptune Console • Navigate to the service using the Find Services search bar or by directly accessing the console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/neptune/. 3. Select the Neptune Cluster • Choose the Amazon Neptune cluster on which you want to enable audit logging. Click on the cluster name to access its details page. 4. Enable Amazon CloudWatch Logs • In the cluster details page, navigate to the Monitoring or Logging section. • Under CloudWatch Logs, click Enable to enable logging for the Neptune cluster. • Select an existing CloudWatch Logs group or create a new one to store the logs. • Choose the appropriate retention period for the logs, considering your compliance and retention requirements. • Click Save or Apply Changes to enable CloudWatch Logs for the Neptune cluster. 5. Configure Log Levels (Optional) • In the cluster details page, navigate to the Configuration or Logging section.  • Under Logging, you may have the option to configure log levels for different components of Neptune, such as query logs or error logs. • Adjust the log levels according to your logging and troubleshooting needs. • Click Apply Changes to save the log level configuration. 6. Review and Analyze Logs • Access the Amazon CloudWatch console by navigating to CloudWatch in the AWS Management Console. • Go to the CloudWatch Logs section and locate the log group associated with your Neptune cluster. • Select the log group and review the logs generated by Neptune. • Analyze the logs for troubleshooting, performance monitoring, or auditing purposes. 7. Set Up Log Metric Filters and Alarms (Optional) • In the CloudWatch console, navigate to Metric Filters in the left-side menu. • Click on Create metric filter to set up filters to extract specific information from the Neptune logs. • Define the filter patterns to capture the desired log events and extract the required metrics. • Configure alarms based on the extracted metrics to trigger notifications or automated actions when specific conditions are met. 8. Regularly Monitor Logs • Continuously monitor the logs generated by Neptune using the CloudWatch Logs console or programmatically using the CloudWatch APIs. • Review the logs regularly to identify any abnormal or suspicious activities. • Set up appropriate notifications or alerts to proactively respond to critical log events.
input_row.impact: Reduces risks of any fraud since worker activity is being monitored and tracked.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
This control involves enabling and verifying audit logging for Amazon Neptune clusters. AWS APIs and boto3 provide the necessary capabilities to automate this audit. The AWS CLI and boto3 SDK have methods for accessing and configuring Amazon Neptune cluster settings, including checking if logging to CloudWatch is enabled.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_neptune_cluster_audit_logging_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS API calls or boto3 SDK methods to use:**
  - Use `boto3` to interact with the AWS Neptune service.
  - Use the `describe_db_clusters` method in the `boto3` Neptune client to retrieve information about Neptune clusters.
  - Check for the presence and status of logging configurations related to CloudWatch.

- **Specific resources to enumerate:**
  - Enumerate all Amazon Neptune clusters within an AWS account and region.

- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - Check each Neptune cluster for the `enabledCloudwatchLogsExports` attribute to ensure that audit logs are configured to be exported to CloudWatch Logs.
  - Confirm that the cluster's audit logging settings include relevant log types (e.g., `audit`) to verify comprehensive logging.

- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - **Pass:** The Neptune cluster has `enabledCloudwatchLogsExports` set to include audit logs.
  - **Fail:** The Neptune cluster does not have audit logs enabled in the `enabledCloudwatchLogsExports` configuration.

- **Example:** 
  - Example boto3 call: `neptune_client.describe_db_clusters(DBClusterIdentifier='your-cluster-id')['DBClusters'][0]['EnabledCloudwatchLogsExports']`
  - Ensure that `audit` is part of the returned list in `EnabledCloudwatchLogsExports`.

This approach ensures that audit logging for Amazon Neptune clusters is properly configured and enabled, supporting compliance with the CIS benchmark.

**SOURCE:** CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
step2_review:
### REVIEW OF AWS COMPLIANCE CONTROL AUDIT ASSESSMENT

#### 1. APPROACH VALIDATION: PARTIALLY_AGREE
- **Manual vs Automated Classification:** The classification as "Automated" is appropriate given the capabilities of AWS APIs and boto3 to programmatically check and validate audit logging configurations for Amazon Neptune clusters.
- **AWS APIs/boto3/CLI Feasibility:** The use of AWS APIs, specifically boto3, is valid for this audit. However, the assessment should also consider AWS Config rules which can automate compliance checks for logging configurations.
- **Considerations:** While the automated approach is feasible, the assessment could benefit from mentioning AWS Config as an alternative or complementary method to ensure continuous compliance monitoring.

#### 2. TECHNICAL ACCURACY: 8
- **Automation Details:** The use of `describe_db_clusters` and checking the `EnabledCloudwatchLogsExports` attribute is technically correct for verifying audit logging configurations.
- **Boto3 Methods/CLI Commands:** The boto3 method `describe_db_clusters` is valid and correctly used in the context of this audit.
- **Program Name:** The program name `aws_neptune_cluster_audit_logging_enabled` is descriptive but could be more specific. Consider including terms like `cloudwatch` or `log_exports` for clarity.
- **Completeness:** The assessment lacks a mention of ensuring the logs are actually being sent to CloudWatch, which could be verified by checking CloudWatch log streams.

#### 3. IMPROVEMENTS:
- **Missing Considerations:** 
  - Include AWS Config as a potential tool for continuous compliance checks.
  - Verify that the logs are not only configured but also actively being sent to CloudWatch by checking log streams.
- **Alternative AWS API Methods:** Consider using AWS CloudWatch Logs API to verify the existence of log streams for Neptune clusters.
- **Program Name Suggestion:** `aws_neptune_cluster_cloudwatch_audit_logging_check` for clarity on what is being checked.

#### 4. CONFIDENCE: MEDIUM
- **Reasoning:** The assessment is generally accurate but lacks some depth in ensuring logs are actively being sent and monitored. The inclusion of AWS Config and CloudWatch Logs verification would increase confidence.

#### 5. FINAL RECOMMENDATION: MODIFY
- **Explanation:** 
  - Include AWS Config as an additional method for compliance checks.
  - Verify log streams in CloudWatch to ensure logs are being sent.
  - Update the program name for clarity.
- **Re-classify:** No reclassification needed, but enhancements to the automated approach are recommended.

This structured review ensures a comprehensive audit approach by leveraging AWS capabilities effectively while also considering continuous compliance monitoring tools.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions agree that this control can be audited programmatically. AWS exposes the necessary Neptune cluster logging configuration via the `describe_db_clusters` API, specifically the `EnabledCloudwatchLogsExports` attribute. This allows for reliable, automated verification of whether audit logging is enabled. While AWS Config could also be used for continuous compliance, direct API checks via boto3/CLI are sufficient for the audit requirement as described.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
aws_neptune_cluster_audit_logging_to_cloudwatch_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- AWS Resource/Service: Amazon Neptune Clusters
- API/CLI Method: boto3 neptune.describe_db_clusters()
- Properties to Check: For each cluster, the `EnabledCloudwatchLogsExports` attribute must include `"audit"`
- Pass Criteria: Every Neptune cluster in the account/region has `"audit"` in its `EnabledCloudwatchLogsExports` list (i.e., audit logs are exported to CloudWatch Logs)
- Fail Criteria: Any Neptune cluster is missing `"audit"` in its `EnabledCloudwatchLogsExports` list (i.e., audit logging is not enabled/exported to CloudWatch Logs)

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
Both opinions agreed on automation. The second opinion suggested a more descriptive program name and mentioned AWS Config as an alternative, but direct API checks are sufficient and more universally applicable for audit purposes. The program name has been made more descriptive as recommended.

**SOURCE:** CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1
